123	// IsValidStatusTransition reports whether a bead may move from `from` to `to`

125	// transitions to/from the empty string (used by Create defaults) are rejected;

3	import (
4		"bytes"

14	
15	// Run evaluates every check in `all` whose AppliesTo filter matches `ctx`,
16	// in parallel, and returns the collected results sorted by check name.
17	//
18	// Each applicable check is run as `sh -c <command>` with the documented
19	// environment variables injected. After execution, the runner reads the
20	// expected ${EvidenceDir}/${name}.json file and parses it into a Result.
21	//
22	// Failure modes that produce status=error:
23	//   - Non-zero process exit.
24	//   - Result file missing.
25	//   - Result file unparseable / unknown status value.
26	//
27	// Run returns an error only for setup failures (e.g. cannot create
28	// EvidenceDir). Per-check failures are reported as Result{Status:error}
29	// in the returned slice.
30	func Run(ctx context.Context, all []Check, ictx InvocationContext) ([]Result, error) {
31		if err := os.MkdirAll(ictx.EvidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
32			return nil, fmt.Errorf("checks: create evidence dir: %w", err)
33		}
34	
35		var applicable []Check
36		for _, c := range all {
37			if c.Applies(ictx) {
38				applicable = append(applicable, c)
39			}
40		}
41	
42		results := make([]Result, len(applicable))
43		var wg sync.WaitGroup
44		for i := range applicable {
45			wg.Add(1)
46			go func(i int) {
47				defer wg.Done()
48				results[i] = runOne(ctx, applicable[i], ictx)
49			}(i)
50		}
51		wg.Wait()
52	
53		sort.Slice(results, func(i, j int) bool { return results[i].Name < results[j].Name })
54		return results, nil
55	}
56	
57	func runOne(ctx context.Context, c Check, ictx InvocationContext) Result {
58		resultPath := filepath.Join(ictx.EvidenceDir, c.Name+".json")
59		// Best-effort cleanup of any stale result file from a prior run so a
60		// check that crashes without writing one is correctly classified as
61		// status=error rather than silently picking up the previous output.
62		_ = os.Remove(resultPath)
63	
64		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-c", c.Command)
65		cmd.Dir = ictx.ProjectRoot
66		cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
67			"BEAD_ID="+ictx.BeadID,
68			"DIFF_BASE="+ictx.DiffBase,
69			"DIFF_HEAD="+ictx.DiffHead,
70			"PROJECT_ROOT="+ictx.ProjectRoot,
71			"EVIDENCE_DIR="+ictx.EvidenceDir,
72			"RUN_ID="+ictx.RunID,
73			"CHECK_NAME="+c.Name,
74		)
75		var stderr bytes.Buffer
76		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
77	
78		runErr := cmd.Run()
79		exitCode := 0
80		if runErr != nil {
81			if ee, ok := runErr.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
82				exitCode = ee.ExitCode()
83			} else {
84				return Result{
85					Name:     c.Name,
86					Status:   StatusError,
87					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q failed to start: %v", c.Name, runErr),
88					ExitCode: -1,
89					Stderr:   stderr.String(),
90				}
91			}
92		}
93	
94		if runErr != nil {
95			return Result{
96				Name:     c.Name,
97				Status:   StatusError,
98				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q exited %d: %s", c.Name, exitCode, trim(stderr.String())),
99				ExitCode: exitCode,
100				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
101			}
102		}
103	
104		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
105		if readErr != nil {
106			return Result{
107				Name:     c.Name,
108				Status:   StatusError,
109				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q produced no result file at %s", c.Name, resultPath),
110				ExitCode: exitCode,
111				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
112			}
113		}
114	
115		var parsed Result
116		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &parsed); err != nil {
117			return Result{
118				Name:     c.Name,
119				Status:   StatusError,
120				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q wrote invalid JSON to %s: %v", c.Name, resultPath, err),
121				ExitCode: exitCode,
122				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
123			}
124		}
125		parsed.Name = c.Name
126		parsed.ExitCode = exitCode
127		parsed.Stderr = stderr.String()
128		switch parsed.Status {
129		case StatusPass, StatusBlock, StatusError:
130			// ok
131		default:
132			return Result{
133				Name:     c.Name,
134				Status:   StatusError,
135				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q returned unknown status %q", c.Name, parsed.Status),
136				ExitCode: exitCode,
137				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
138			}
139		}
140		return parsed
141	}

143	func trim(s string) string {
144		const max = 500
145		if len(s) <= max {
146			return s
147		}
148		return s[:max] + "…"
149	}

1	// Package checks implements the language-agnostic pre-merge check protocol.
2	//
3	// A check is a project-declared mechanical gate evaluated before a worker's
4	// worktree is merged back. Each check is one YAML file under
5	// .ddx/checks/*.yaml describing a command to run and the situations it
6	// applies to (path globs, bead labels). The check command is invoked as a
7	// subprocess with the following environment variables injected:
8	//
9	//	BEAD_ID       — the bead being evaluated
10	//	DIFF_BASE     — git revision the work started from
11	//	DIFF_HEAD     — current worktree HEAD
12	//	PROJECT_ROOT  — absolute path to the project root
13	//	EVIDENCE_DIR  — directory the check must write its result file into
14	//	RUN_ID        — unique identifier for this evaluation run
15	//
16	// The check writes its result to ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with shape:
17	//
18	//	{
19	//	  "status":   "pass" | "block" | "error",
20	//	  "message":  "<human-readable summary>",
21	//	  "violations": [
22	//	    {"file": "...", "line": 0, "symbol": "...", "kind": "...", "detail": "..."}
23	//	  ]
24	//	}
25	//
26	// Exit code 0 indicates the check ran to completion (the status field in the
27	// result file is authoritative). A non-zero exit, a missing result file, or
28	// an unparseable result file is treated as status=error.
29	package checks
30	
31	// Status enumerates the three possible check outcomes.
32	type Status string
33	
34	const (
35		StatusPass  Status = "pass"
36		StatusBlock Status = "block"
37		StatusError Status = "error"
38	)
39	
40	// HookPreMerge is the only currently supported lifecycle hook.
41	const HookPreMerge = "pre_merge"
42	
43	// Check is the parsed representation of one .ddx/checks/*.yaml file.
44	type Check struct {
45		// Name uniquely identifies the check; also the basename of its result
46		// file (${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${Name}.json). Required.
47		Name string `yaml:"name"`
48		// Command is the shell command to execute. Required. Run via
49		// `sh -c <command>` so projects can use pipes/redirection.
50		Command string `yaml:"command"`
51		// When is the lifecycle hook the check binds to. Currently only
52		// "pre_merge" is supported. Required.
53		When string `yaml:"when"`
54		// AppliesTo gates whether this check runs for a given (paths, labels)
55		// invocation context. Zero value means "always".
56		AppliesTo AppliesTo `yaml:"applies_to"`
57	
58		// SourceFile records the on-disk path the check was loaded from
59		// (used for diagnostics). Not part of the YAML schema.
60		SourceFile string `yaml:"-"`
61	}
62	
63	// AppliesTo declares the conditions under which a check is evaluated.
64	//
65	// Semantics:
66	//   - If neither Paths nor Labels is set, the check ALWAYS applies.
67	//   - If Paths is set, the check applies when any changed path matches
68	//     any glob (filepath.Match semantics, evaluated against the path
69	//     relative to PROJECT_ROOT).
70	//   - If Labels is set, the check applies when the bead carries any of
71	//     the listed labels.
72	//   - If both are set, EITHER a matching path OR a matching label causes
73	//     the check to apply (logical OR).
74	type AppliesTo struct {
75		Paths  []string `yaml:"paths,omitempty"`
76		Labels []string `yaml:"labels,omitempty"`
77	}
78	
79	// Result is the parsed outcome of running one check.
80	type Result struct {
81		Name       string      `json:"name"`
82		Status     Status      `json:"status"`
83		Message    string      `json:"message,omitempty"`
84		Violations []Violation `json:"violations,omitempty"`
85	
86		// ExitCode and Stderr are captured by the runner for diagnostics
87		// when status=error. Not part of the on-disk JSON contract.
88		ExitCode int    `json:"-"`
89		Stderr   string `json:"-"`
90	}
91	
92	// Violation describes one offending location reported by a check.
93	type Violation struct {
94		File   string `json:"file,omitempty"`
95		Line   int    `json:"line,omitempty"`
96		Symbol string `json:"symbol,omitempty"`
97		Kind   string `json:"kind,omitempty"`
98		Detail string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
99	}
100	
101	// InvocationContext bundles the runtime values injected as environment
102	// variables into each check command, plus the bead labels and changed
103	// paths used to evaluate AppliesTo filtering.
104	type InvocationContext struct {
105		BeadID       string
106		DiffBase     string
107		DiffHead     string
108		ProjectRoot  string
109		EvidenceDir  string
110		RunID        string
111		BeadLabels   []string
112		ChangedPaths []string
113	}

```go
    Violations []Violation `json:"violations,omitempty"`
}
```

**Check execution:**
- Checks load from `.ddx/checks/*.yaml` files
- Run as `sh -c <command>` with injected env vars (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, etc.)
- Checks write JSON results to `${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json`
- Run in parallel with sync.WaitGroup, sorted by name

### 7. Testing Patterns (`cli/cmd/*_test.go`)

**From `bead_acceptance_test.go`:**
```go
func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
    t.Helper()
    t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
    t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", "")
    return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
    workingDir := t.TempDir()
    factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
    rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
    
    createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", ...)
    // assertions with testify/require and testify/assert
}
```

**Testing approach:**
- Uses `t.TempDir()` for isolation
- Sets env vars via `t.Setenv()`
- Creates fresh CommandFactory per test
- Helper function `executeCommand()` wraps cobra execution
- Uses `testify/require` for setup assertions, `testify/assert` for validations
- JSON output parsed with `json.Unmarshal` for verification

### 8. Module Information (`cli/go.mod`)

```

go 1.26.2

Key dependencies:
- github.com/spf13/cobra v1.10.2          (CLI framework)
- github.com/spf13/viper v1.21.0          (Configuration)
- gopkg.in/yaml.v3 v3.0.1                  (YAML parsing)
- github.com/stretchr/testify v1.11.1      (Testing)
- github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go   (Claude API)
```

### Summary

The DDx CLI uses a clean **factory pattern** where:
1. `CommandFactory` encapsulates command creation and dependency injection
2. Methods like `(f *CommandFactory) newXxxCommand()` create fresh `*cobra.Command` instances
3. Subcommands are explicitly registered in parent commands via `AddCommand()`
4. Internal packages provide domain logic (`bead` for task tracking, `checks` for validation gates)
5. Testing uses environment isolation and fresh factory instances per test
6. The existing `checks/` package is already well-established with YAML loading, subprocess execution, and JSON result parsing

The codebase demonstrates a mature, testable architecture suitable for extensibility.

1	// Package checks implements the language-agnostic pre-merge check protocol.
2	//
3	// A check is a project-declared mechanical gate evaluated before a worker's
4	// worktree is merged back. Each check is one YAML file under
5	// .ddx/checks/*.yaml describing a command to run and the situations it
6	// applies to (path globs, bead labels). The check command is invoked as a
7	// subprocess with the following environment variables injected:
8	//
9	//	BEAD_ID       — the bead being evaluated
10	//	DIFF_BASE     — git revision the work started from
11	//	DIFF_HEAD     — current worktree HEAD
12	//	PROJECT_ROOT  — absolute path to the project root
13	//	EVIDENCE_DIR  — directory the check must write its result file into
14	//	RUN_ID        — unique identifier for this evaluation run
15	//
16	// The check writes its result to ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with shape:
17	//
18	//	{
19	//	  "status":   "pass" | "block" | "error",
20	//	  "message":  "<human-readable summary>",
21	//	  "violations": [
22	//	    {"file": "...", "line": 0, "symbol": "...", "kind": "...", "detail": "..."}
23	//	  ]
24	//	}
25	//
26	// Exit code 0 indicates the check ran to completion (the status field in the
27	// result file is authoritative). A non-zero exit, a missing result file, or
28	// an unparseable result file is treated as status=error.
29	package checks
30	
31	// Status enumerates the three possible check outcomes.
32	type Status string
33	
34	const (
35		StatusPass  Status = "pass"
36		StatusBlock Status = "block"
37		StatusError Status = "error"
38	)
39	
40	// HookPreMerge is the only currently supported lifecycle hook.
41	const HookPreMerge = "pre_merge"
42	
43	// Check is the parsed representation of one .ddx/checks/*.yaml file.
44	type Check struct {
45		// Name uniquely identifies the check; also the basename of its result
46		// file (${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${Name}.json). Required.
47		Name string `yaml:"name"`
48		// Command is the shell command to execute. Required. Run via
49		// `sh -c <command>` so projects can use pipes/redirection.
50		Command string `yaml:"command"`
51		// When is the lifecycle hook the check binds to. Currently only
52		// "pre_merge" is supported. Required.
53		When string `yaml:"when"`
54		// AppliesTo gates whether this check runs for a given (paths, labels)
55		// invocation context. Zero value means "always".
56		AppliesTo AppliesTo `yaml:"applies_to"`
57	
58		// SourceFile records the on-disk path the check was loaded from
59		// (used for diagnostics). Not part of the YAML schema.
60		SourceFile string `yaml:"-"`
61	}
62	
63	// AppliesTo declares the conditions under which a check is evaluated.
64	//
65	// Semantics:
66	//   - If neither Paths nor Labels is set, the check ALWAYS applies.
67	//   - If Paths is set, the check applies when any changed path matches
68	//     any glob (filepath.Match semantics, evaluated against the path
69	//     relative to PROJECT_ROOT).
70	//   - If Labels is set, the check applies when the bead carries any of
71	//     the listed labels.
72	//   - If both are set, EITHER a matching path OR a matching label causes
73	//     the check to apply (logical OR).
74	type AppliesTo struct {
75		Paths  []string `yaml:"paths,omitempty"`
76		Labels []string `yaml:"labels,omitempty"`
77	}
78	
79	// Result is the parsed outcome of running one check.
80	type Result struct {

82		Status     Status      `json:"status"`

1	package checks
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"os/exec"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sort"
12		"sync"
13	)
14	
15	// Run evaluates every check in `all` whose AppliesTo filter matches `ctx`,
16	// in parallel, and returns the collected results sorted by check name.
17	//
18	// Each applicable check is run as `sh -c <command>` with the documented
19	// environment variables injected. After execution, the runner reads the
20	// expected ${EvidenceDir}/${name}.json file and parses it into a Result.
21	//
22	// Failure modes that produce status=error:
23	//   - Non-zero process exit.
24	//   - Result file missing.
25	//   - Result file unparseable / unknown status value.
26	//
27	// Run returns an error only for setup failures (e.g. cannot create
28	// EvidenceDir). Per-check failures are reported as Result{Status:error}
29	// in the returned slice.
30	func Run(ctx context.Context, all []Check, ictx InvocationContext) ([]Result, error) {
31		if err := os.MkdirAll(ictx.EvidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
32			return nil, fmt.Errorf("checks: create evidence dir: %w", err)
33		}
34	
35		var applicable []Check
36		for _, c := range all {
37			if c.Applies(ictx) {
38				applicable = append(applicable, c)
39			}
40		}
41	
42		results := make([]Result, len(applicable))
43		var wg sync.WaitGroup
44		for i := range applicable {
45			wg.Add(1)
46			go func(i int) {
47				defer wg.Done()
48				results[i] = runOne(ctx, applicable[i], ictx)
49			}(i)
50		}
51		wg.Wait()
52	
53		sort.Slice(results, func(i, j int) bool { return results[i].Name < results[j].Name })
54		return results, nil
55	}
56	
57	func runOne(ctx context.Context, c Check, ictx InvocationContext) Result {
58		resultPath := filepath.Join(ictx.EvidenceDir, c.Name+".json")
59		// Best-effort cleanup of any stale result file from a prior run so a
60		// check that crashes without writing one is correctly classified as
61		// status=error rather than silently picking up the previous output.
62		_ = os.Remove(resultPath)
63	
64		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-c", c.Command)
65		cmd.Dir = ictx.ProjectRoot
66		cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
67			"BEAD_ID="+ictx.BeadID,
68			"DIFF_BASE="+ictx.DiffBase,
69			"DIFF_HEAD="+ictx.DiffHead,
70			"PROJECT_ROOT="+ictx.ProjectRoot,
71			"EVIDENCE_DIR="+ictx.EvidenceDir,
72			"RUN_ID="+ictx.RunID,
73			"CHECK_NAME="+c.Name,
74		)
75		var stderr bytes.Buffer
76		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
77	
78		runErr := cmd.Run()
79		exitCode := 0
80		if runErr != nil {
81			if ee, ok := runErr.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
82				exitCode = ee.ExitCode()
83			} else {
84				return Result{
85					Name:     c.Name,
86					Status:   StatusError,
87					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q failed to start: %v", c.Name, runErr),
88					ExitCode: -1,
89					Stderr:   stderr.String(),
90				}
91			}
92		}
93	
94		if runErr != nil {
95			return Result{
96				Name:     c.Name,
97				Status:   StatusError,
98				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q exited %d: %s", c.Name, exitCode, trim(stderr.String())),
99				ExitCode: exitCode,
100				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
101			}
102		}
103	
104		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
105		if readErr != nil {
106			return Result{
107				Name:     c.Name,
108				Status:   StatusError,
109				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q produced no result file at %s", c.Name, resultPath),
110				ExitCode: exitCode,
111				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
112			}
113		}
114	
115		var parsed Result
116		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &parsed); err != nil {
117			return Result{
118				Name:     c.Name,
119				Status:   StatusError,
120				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q wrote invalid JSON to %s: %v", c.Name, resultPath, err),
121				ExitCode: exitCode,
122				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
123			}
124		}
125		parsed.Name = c.Name
126		parsed.ExitCode = exitCode
127		parsed.Stderr = stderr.String()
128		switch parsed.Status {
129		case StatusPass, StatusBlock, StatusError:
130			// ok
131		default:
132			return Result{
133				Name:     c.Name,
134				Status:   StatusError,
135				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q returned unknown status %q", c.Name, parsed.Status),
136				ExitCode: exitCode,
137				Stderr:   stderr.String(),
138			}
139		}
140		return parsed
141	}
142	
143	func trim(s string) string {
144		const max = 500
145		if len(s) <= max {
146			return s
147		}
148		return s[:max] + "…"
149	}

    - internal/checks/protocol.go: Check, AppliesTo, Result, Violation, Status
      (pass|block|error), InvocationContext; full schema + I/O contract in
      package doc.
    - internal/checks/loader.go: LoadDir/LoadFile read .ddx/checks/*.yaml with
      KnownFields(true); reject malformed YAML, unknown fields, missing
      required fields, and duplicate names with file:line context.
    - internal/checks/runner.go: parallel sh -c execution with documented env
      injection (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR,
      RUN_ID, CHECK_NAME); reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; non-zero exit,
      missing file, invalid JSON, and unknown status all map to status=error.
    - cmd/ac.go: ddx ac run <bead-id> resolves bead, computes DIFF_BASE
      (--from > bead.Extra base_rev > merge-base with main) and DIFF_HEAD,
      loads checks, writes evidence under .ddx/executions/ac-<runID>/, prints
      per-check status with violations, exits non-zero on block/error.
      Supports --check <name> for targeted iteration.
    
    NO integration with execute-bead loop yet — that is sibling slice
    ddx-3e60fd84.
